kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: A strong overall percentile (94th of 26,279 historical Shaman parses on this encounter) sits alongside underperformance against our own curated Top 100 bracket — HPS (800) trails that elite sample's median (1012) by roughly 21%, and active time (69.3%) runs well below the sample's ~86% norm. The two aren't contradictory: 94th percentile reflects the entire historical population, a far less selective bar than the Top 100 comparison used throughout the rest of this page. Overheal (33.2%) sits comfortably between the Top 100's best (15.7%) and median (35.3%), not a concern on its own.
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The two Chain Heal ranks Vajomee actually cast run in the same direction as the Top 100 sample but with a real efficiency gap: the costlier guid 25423 (540 mana) is Vajomee's primary at 46.2%, broadly in line with the sample's own primary (61.3%), but the cheaper guid 10623 (405 mana) makes up 37.4% of Vajomee's healing versus just 3.2% of the Top 100 mix — over 10x the benchmark's usage of that rank. The sample also spreads healing across three lower Chain Heal ranks (guids 25422/10622/1064, 435/315/260 mana, 5.8%/4.0%/3.8% combined) and Healing Stream Totem (3.5%) that Vajomee never cast at all this kill — a real, checkable gap, not noise.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Earth Shield stayed almost entirely on Blockarina and Captinhelp (3 and 2 casts respectively), both of whom later died this kill. Blockarina's shield had last been reapplied 53s before its death at ~342s into the fight — still active, but not enough against whatever damage landed; Captinhelp's death near the very end (~480s, 5s before the kill) came 77s after its last Earth Shield refresh, too stale a gap to call a direct link. More notable: Ancestral Swiftness was cast at ~340s, just 1.9s before a 4-player death cluster (Blockarina, Ghostpepperx, Vinnyvozz, Quruella all within ~5s of each other) — consistent with a reactive emergency cast into a burst-damage event the healer couldn't fully cover, not a wasted cooldown. No flask or elixir was active at pull start (Top 100: 70% had one) — a real, fixable gap, and a change from the 07-10 raid night where flask was up on all 9 kills with an otherwise identical loadout (see the raid overview). HPM (6.46) trails the Top 100 average (7.45) and median (7.37) by a moderate margin, consistent with the missing flask.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (50.7% vs. the Top 100's 49.1%, +1.6) and top-target concentration (18.9% vs. 18.5%, +0.4) both land within the roughly ±5-point noise band established across this pipeline's other pulls — a normal, unremarkable target spread that tracks the sample average closely. Blockarina and Captinhelp, the top two recipients here, are the same two players Earth Shield was kept on (see above).

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: identical, item ID for item ID, to the full 9-kill gear audit from the 07-10-2026 raid night just two days earlier
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: Boots and cloak are the same two real, fixable enchant gaps carried over from 07-10 — both epic-quality items, both mechanically eligible for a permanent enchant (e.g. Boots: Cat's Swiftness/Surefooted; Cloak: Subtlety/major resistance) in this era. The missing flask is new this pull, not a carryover gap — the same character had one active on all 9 kills two days prior, so this reads as a one-off missed consumable for this specific pull rather than a standing gear issue.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: Only one boss this raid night, so there's no cross-kill pattern to report — see the full breakdown on the boss page itself. Percentile (94th) is strong against the full historical population, but HPS and active time both trail this pipeline's own curated Top 100 Kael'thas sample by a real margin (see the boss page's scorecard section); the missing flask this pull is the most likely single contributor.

GearCheckItems.Description: No flask or elixir active at pull start, unlike every kill on the 07-10 raid night
Well Fed food buff active at pull start
